select_when: The problem is too tangled to reason about as a whole and teams keep mixing symptoms, causes, owners, and options in one blob.
Progress depends on identifying which branches are causal, which are descriptive, and which are actually actionable.
The breakdown should hand off into priorities, owners, sequencing, and the few branches worth pursuing rather than ending as a neat tree on paper.
High-volume information needs to be organized into a structure that reveals the key drivers instead of leaving facts isolated and unusable.
avoid_when: A familiar framework is being forced onto a novel problem just because it is the easiest cut available.
The tree is creating false certainty by making the structure look tidy even though the causal landscape may still be wrong.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the curation centered on problem disaggregation that changes action, not on abstract framework admiration.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor generalized issue-tree libraries because the raw source already gives cleaner MECE and branch-selection discipline.
Did not widen into higher-order composition source files in this run.
open_questions: The output label compresses several raw structures such as logic trees, issue pyramids, and branch pruning into one operational form.

select_when.note: The raw file contains explicit activation bullets and nearby strengths around complexity, driver isolation, and execution handoff; review mainly tightens them into routing triggers.

avoid_when.note: The second condition is explicit in raw danger form and the first is a conservative anti-activation compression of the familiar-framework warning.

input_type.note: The source clearly applies to tangled problem structures, but the compact machine-usable label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The raw model builds MECE trees and issue structures that expose action branches; the output label compresses that into an operational artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Decomposition is strongly deductive and systems-oriented in use, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
